bug_fix_report.reproduction: After a cache cleanup, Tenant A warms the reassignment-policy cache for project-42, then Tenant B requests its reassignment policy; test_reassignment_is_tenant_isolated deterministically fails because Tenant B receives Tenant A's policy instead of its own.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: The reassignment-policy cache key contains only the project id and omits tenant identity, causing Tenant A and Tenant B to share reassignment-policy:project-42.
confirming_evidence: Logging only the computed cache key showed that both tenant requests used reassignment-policy:project-42, and source inspection confirmed that the key contained only the project id.
With tenant id included in the cache key, the original cross-tenant reproduction passed for 50 consecutive paired requests and the surrounding policy tests passed.
When only the cache-key change was removed, the original cross-tenant failure returned on the first paired request.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Include tenant id in the reassignment-policy cache key while leaving authorization code unchanged.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Retain test_reassignment_is_tenant_isolated and hand the verified boundary to pc-tdd.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The authorization guard was bypassed because the cleanup moved it.
debug_journal.prediction: The guard trace will be absent for Tenant B.
debug_journal.experiment: Add a trace at guard entry without changing code behavior.
debug_journal.result: The guard ran and authorized Tenant B correctly, so the hypothesis was rejected and no authorization patch was applied.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The cache key omits tenant identity.
debug_journal.prediction: The same cache entry id will appear for Tenant A and Tenant B.
debug_journal.experiment: Log only the computed cache key.
debug_journal.result: Both requests used reassignment-policy:project-42, and source inspection confirmed that the key contained only the project id.
